sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea (2013) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kobun Shizuno's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Battle Royale
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea clicked for you, Battle Royale (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea clicked for you, Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea?
faq.answer: Try Battle Royale, Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle, Case Closed: Countdown to Heaven—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea (2013) is best described as Animation, Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: Private Eye in the Distant Sea part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
